spans.text:
Course Format and Expectations:
We meet twice a week, and most weeks I will present a new lesson in the first session and you’ll do
some work on a short assignment in the second session. I’ll coach each of you as you work, and then
we’ll all engage in a group critique of everyone’s progress. You’ll generally have some prep work to do
on your own before each workshop session and then some finish-up work to do afterwards. Plan to
spend about five hours outside of class each week working on your own with the equipment and
software I’ll be teaching you. Your overall development as you move through these short assignments
will account for 50% of your course grade.
As we move into the second half of the semester, I’ll begin asking you to work on your final project in
phases and bring new material to our weekly workshop sessions for coaching and critique. Your final
project will account for 30% of your course grade.
The remaining 20% of your course grade will be based on your attendance and participation. Due to
the hands-on nature of this class, attendance and participation in-person is mandatory. Unexcused
absences will negatively impact your course grade; please communicate with me right away in the
event of an illness or emergency that makes it impossible for you to attend class so that we can figure
out a way to get you up to speed about what you’ll miss. Moreover, for full participation credit you must
arrive prepared for each of our workshops and actively engage in our critique sessions.
spans.label: ASSIGN

spans.text:
Tentative Course Plan (check our LATTE course page regularly for updates):
DATE CLASS SESSION
29-AUG PHOTOGRAPHY, PART 1
Compositional techniques (subject/background, balance, point of view, simplicity).
Controlling focus. Depth of field. Working a shot.
2-SEP PHOTOGRAPHY, PART 2
Image editing in Lightroom (cropping and retouching). Critique photos.
5-SEP PHOTOGRAPHY, PART 3
Shutter Priority mode. Controlling exposure (aperture, shutter, ISO). Shooting
moving subjects. Evoking a scene.
9-SEP NARRATIVE SLIDESHOW EDITING, PART 1
Exporting from Lightroom. Assembling an image sequence in Premiere.
12-SEP NARRATIVE SLIDESHOW EDITING, PART 2
Adding effects & keyframes. Spotting in sound effects and basic audio editing.
16-SEP NARRATIVE SLIDESHOW EDITING, PART 3
Critique slideshows. Audio recording techniques (for sound effects). Writing a
prompt for a podcast interview.
19-SEP AUDIO STORYTELLING, PART 1
Interviewing techniques. In-class interview practice. Creating a transcript.

23-SEP No class – Rosh Hashanah
26-SEP AUDIO STORYTELLING, PART 2
Logging audio with markers. Editing and arranging sound bites in Audition.
Creating a story structure.
30-SEP AUDIO STORYTELLING, PART 3
Writing and voicing narration. Sound design.
3-OCT LOOKING AHEAD AND LOOKING BACK
Critique audio stories. Sound design workshop.
7-OCT No class - Sukkot
10-OCT PHOTOGRAPHY, PART 4
Shooting in fully manual mode. Adjusting aperture. Depth of field.
(Tue) 14-OCT No class – Shmini Atzeret
(Thu) 16-OCT VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 1
Breaking down an action into shots and takes. Assembling a sequence.
(Fri) 17-OCT REVIEW FEEDBACK FOR PODCAST DRAFTS; REVIEW VIDEO SHOOTING
21-OCT VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 2
Shooting a talking head.
24-OCT VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 3
Editing sound bites from a talking head interview. Adding B-roll.
28-OCT AUDIO STORYTELLING, PART 4
Workshop first interview for final project. Creating narrative structure and drama.
31-OCT VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 4
Workshop B-roll and talking head sound bites from shoot with partners.
4-NOV AUDIO STORYTELLING, PART 5
Workshop edited interview. Discuss narration and sound design.
7-NOV VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 5
Workshop second interview and B-roll for final project. Cutting on action and
movement. Alternating angles and viewpoints. Intercutting. Emphasizing
character, story, and emotion.
11-NOV VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 6
Editing picture and sound to match your script.
14-NOV VIDEO PRODUCTION, PART 7
Trimming and fine-tuning your edit. Using split edits. Reframing. Match frame.
Adding natural sound.
18-NOV FINAL PROJECT WORKSHOP SESSION 1
Workshop third interview for final project.
21-NOV FINAL PROJECT WORKSHOP SESSION 2
Workshop overall structure of final project.
25-NOV FINAL PROJECT WORKSHOP SESSION 3

28-NOV No class – Thanksgiving Break
2-DEC FINAL PROJECT WORKSHOP SESSION 4
5-DEC FINAL PROJECT WORKSHOP SESSION 5
TBD FINAL PRESENTATION SESSION
spans.label: SCHEDULE

spans.text:

spans.label: GRADE

spans.text:
Accommodations:
Please talk with me as soon as possible if I can help work together with you and Student Accessibility
Support to provide accommodations that will help you succeed in this class. If you already have an
accommodation letter from SAS, please send me a copy right away. You can find helpful student FAQs
and resources on the SAS website.
spans.label: MATERIAL

spans.text:
It is important that you complete all of your work using the equipment and software I will be teaching
you. Your ability to communicate effectively will depend upon your technical facility with this equipment
and software; standardizing it is the best way for me to ensure that you have all of the technical skills
you will need to do good work.
Our post-production software is available on all of the computers in both of the labs at Sound and
Image Media Studios (SIMS). These labs have extensive operating hours but are not open 24/7, so
please plan your editing time accordingly. Members of the Sound and Image Media Studios (SIMS)
student staff are on duty to provide refresher training on the equipment and software as you need it,
and to help you troubleshoot.
Though there will be plenty of help available with the equipment and software we will be using, you
should prepare yourself to encounter unexpected problems that may slow down your work--technology
is like that. Please plan ahead so that you have adequate time to meet all weekly deadlines--if work is
due in class and you arrive without it, you will miss the chance to receive valuable feedback and you
will not receive full credit for participating in that class.
spans.label: MATERIAL
